triples.subject: European carmakers
triples.sentence: In 2023, European carmakers accounted for 60% of electric car sales in the region, compared to over 80% in 2015.

triples.subject: Volkswagen, Stellantis and BMW
triples.object: 45% of European electric car sales in 2023
triples.sentence: Volkswagen, Stellantis and BMW aggregated to 45% of European electric car sales in 2023, but competition is getting tougher among front-runners.

triples.subject: Renault-Nissan-Mitsubishi Alliance
triples.object: 7% of European electric car sales in 2023
triples.sentence: Renault-Nissan-Mitsubishi Alliance accounted for nearly 40% of European electric car sales in 2015, but just 7% in 2023.

triples.subject: Ford
triples.sentence: Ford estimates losses on EV investments of up to USD 5.5 billion in 2023, and expects even tougher future competition from more affordable Tesla models and Chinese carmakers.

triples.subject: Start-ups developing EV charging technology
triples.object: over USD 400 million in early-stage VC
triples.sentence: Start-ups developing EV charging technology attracted over USD 400 million in early-stage VC, and battery and battery component makers USD 260 million, together accounting for half of early-stage VC.

triples.subject: Numbat
triples.object: USD 75 million in series A funding
triples.sentence: Similarly, German EV charging start-up Numbat raised USD 75 million in series A funding through the European Infrastructure Fund, as well as another USD 75 million in loans.

triples.subject: Charge Zone
triples.sentence: Indian start-up Charge Zone also raised nearly USD 55 million to develop nearly 300 charging stations.

triples.subject: Northvolt
triples.object: USD 400 million from Ontario’s asset manager
triples.sentence: To support its global expansion, Swedish battery maker Northvolt raised USD 400 million from Ontario’s asset manager, USD 1.2 billion and then another USD 150 million in debt from public Canadian investors and private banks, a USD 740 million grant from the German Government, and a USD 5 billion loan from the European Investment Bank and Nordic Investment Bank.
